sourceEvidence: slam_toolbox supports loop closure to produce loop-closed, globally consistent maps.

example: Map-based localization works well in static environments.
exampleZh: map-based localization 在静态环境中效果很好。
sourceEvidence: AMCL provides map-based localization by matching laser scans against a known occupancy grid.

example: The point cloud is too noisy for registration.
exampleZh: 这个 point cloud 对 registration 来说太 noisy 了。
sourceEvidence: PCL filtering removes outliers and unwanted points from noisy point cloud data.

example: The LiDAR scan is too sparse at long range for reliable detection.
exampleZh: LiDAR scan 在远距离太 sparse 了，无法可靠检测。
sourceEvidence: Downsampling is used to reduce dense point clouds; sparse point clouds may not need it.

example: A dense point cloud gives better registration results but costs more CPU.
exampleZh: dense point cloud 能给出更好的 registration 结果但消耗更多 CPU。
sourceEvidence: Dense point clouds can be downsampled to reduce computational load while preserving structure.

example: Pass the filtered point cloud to the segmentation node.
exampleZh: 把 filtered point cloud 传给 segmentation node。
sourceEvidence: After applying a PassThrough or StatisticalOutlierRemoval filter, the filtered point cloud contains only relevant points.

example: Cluster the segmented points into individual objects.
exampleZh: 把 segmented 的点 cluster 成单独的物体。
sourceEvidence: The segmented point cloud separates the ground plane from above-ground objects.

example: The object is partially occluded in this camera view.
exampleZh: 在这个 camera view 中物体被部分 occluded 了。
sourceEvidence: Registration can fail when large portions of the scene are occluded between consecutive scans.

example: Segmentation is harder in a cluttered scene with many overlapping objects.
exampleZh: 在有许多重叠物体的 cluttered scene 中 segmentation 更难。
sourceEvidence: Clustering algorithms must handle cluttered scenes where objects are close together.

example: Make sure the camera is calibrated before running visual SLAM.
exampleZh: 运行 visual SLAM 前确保 camera 已经被 calibrated。
sourceEvidence: A calibrated camera provides undistorted images and known intrinsic parameters.

example: Check if the two scans are well aligned after ICP.
exampleZh: 检查 ICP 后两个 scan 是否已 well aligned。
sourceEvidence: After registration, the two point clouds are aligned in a common reference frame.

example: The camera frame looks misaligned with the LiDAR frame.
exampleZh: camera frame 和 LiDAR frame 之间看起来 misaligned。
sourceEvidence: Without proper calibration, depth and RGB images may be misaligned, causing fusion errors.

example: Add the registered scan to the global map.
exampleZh: 把 registered scan 加到 global map 里。
sourceEvidence: A registered point cloud has been transformed to align with the target reference.

example: The tracked features should be evenly distributed across the image.
exampleZh: tracked feature 应该均匀分布在图像中。
sourceEvidence: Tracked features across frames are used to estimate camera motion in visual SLAM.

example: Use the calibration parameters to undistort the distorted image.
exampleZh: 用 calibration parameter 矫正 distorted image。
sourceEvidence: Camera calibration corrects distorted images by estimating radial and tangential distortion coefficients.

shortMeaningInSentence: in the sensor field of view
example: Only visible objects are included in the costmap.
exampleZh: 只有 visible 的物体才会被纳入 costmap。
sourceEvidence: The planning scene represents all visible objects known to the robot from sensor data.

example: Test the controller in the simulated environment before deploying on hardware.
exampleZh: 在 simulated 环境中测试 controller 再部署到硬件上。
sourceEvidence: Gazebo provides a simulated environment with physics, sensors, and robot models for testing.

example: We need to close the sim-to-real gap for this grasping task.
exampleZh: 我们需要缩小这个 grasping task 的 sim-to-real gap。
sourceEvidence: Gazebo is designed to support sim-to-real transfer by providing realistic physics and sensor models.

example: Record a rosbag to make the bug reproducible.
exampleZh: 录一个 rosbag 让这个 bug reproducible。
sourceEvidence: rosbag2 enables reproducible testing by recording and replaying exact sensor data streams.

example: Run the perception pipeline offline on the recorded rosbag.
exampleZh: 在录制的 rosbag 上 offline 运行 perception pipeline。
sourceEvidence: rosbag2 supports offline analysis by recording data that can be processed later.

example: The online planner must respond within the control loop period.
exampleZh: online planner 必须在 control loop 周期内响应。
sourceTitle: ROS2 About Quality of Service Settings
sourceEvidence: Online operation requires QoS settings that balance reliability and low latency for live sensor streams.

example: The embedded controller runs at 1 kHz on the motor board.
exampleZh: embedded controller 在 motor board 上以 1 kHz 运行。
sourceEvidence: ros2_control hardware interfaces connect ROS2 to embedded motor controllers and sensor boards.

example: The mechanical design should match the URDF exactly.
exampleZh: mechanical design 应该和 URDF 完全匹配。
sourceEvidence: URDF describes the mechanical structure of a robot including links, joints, and their properties.

example: The diagnostics aggregator flagged the IMU as faulty.
exampleZh: diagnostics aggregator 将 IMU 标记为 faulty。
sourceEvidence: The ROS2 diagnostics system monitors components and reports faulty hardware or software states.

example: Check if the subscriber is connected to the publisher.
exampleZh: 检查 subscriber 是否已 connected 到 publisher。
sourceEvidence: When a publisher and subscriber are connected, messages flow from the publisher to the subscriber via DDS.

example: The sensor node appears disconnected from the rest of the system.
exampleZh: sensor node 看起来和系统其他部分 disconnected。
sourceEvidence: If a subscriber becomes disconnected, it will stop receiving messages until the connection is re-established.

example: Build a sensor-driven pipeline that adapts to available data.
exampleZh: 构建一个 sensor-driven 的 pipeline，能适应可用数据。
sourceEvidence: robot_localization fuses multiple sensor inputs for sensor-driven state estimation.

example: The planner should be robust to sensor noise and dynamic obstacles.
exampleZh: planner 应该对 sensor noise 和 dynamic obstacle 是 robust 的。
sourceEvidence: SLAM systems are evaluated for robust performance across diverse environments and sensor degradation.

example: A data-driven approach may outperform hand-tuned heuristics.
exampleZh: data-driven 的方法可能优于手动调参的启发式。
sourceEvidence: Modern SLAM research increasingly uses data-driven methods alongside classical geometric approaches.

example: The trained object detector runs at 15 Hz on the robot.
exampleZh: trained object detector 在机器人上以 15 Hz 运行。
sourceEvidence: PCL includes tools for evaluating trained models on point cloud classification tasks.

example: The connection drop is intermittent and hard to reproduce.
exampleZh: 连接断开是 intermittent 的，很难复现。
sourceEvidence: Logging at DEBUG level helps diagnose intermittent failures that are hard to reproduce reliably.

example: Every critical component should be observable through diagnostics.
exampleZh: 每个关键组件都应该通过 diagnostics 变得 observable。
sourceEvidence: The diagnostics system makes component health observable through standardized status messages.

example: The logged data shows the planner took 45 ms per cycle.
exampleZh: logged 数据显示 planner 每个 cycle 用了 45 ms。
sourceEvidence: All logged messages include severity level, timestamp, and source node information.

example: Check if the QoS profiles are compatible between the two nodes.
exampleZh: 检查两个 node 之间的 QoS profile 是否 compatible。
sourceEvidence: Publisher and subscriber QoS settings must be compatible for DDS to establish communication.

shortMeaningInSentence: still works but will be removed
example: This API is deprecated and will be removed in the next release.
exampleZh: 这个 API 已 deprecated，将在下个版本中移除。
sourceEvidence: ROS2 marks older APIs as deprecated before removing them in future releases.

example: Check which controllers are available before switching.
exampleZh: 切换前检查哪些 controller 是 available 的。
sourceEvidence: The controller manager lists all available controllers that are loaded and can be activated.

example: This node should publish a message every second.
exampleZh: 这个 node 应该每秒 publish a message。
sourceEvidence: ROS2 topics allow nodes to publish messages that other nodes can subscribe to.

example: Subscribe to a topic like the local costmap to receive updates.
exampleZh: subscribe to a topic 比如 local costmap 来接收更新。
sourceEvidence: Nodes can subscribe to topics to receive messages published by other nodes in the ROS2 graph.

example: The node should call a service before starting the task.
exampleZh: node 应该在开始任务前 call a service。
sourceEvidence: ROS2 services use a call-and-response model: a client calls a service and waits for the response.

shortMeaningInSentence: make a service or action request
example: Send a request to the map server to get the occupancy grid.
exampleZh: Send a request 到 map server 获取 occupancy grid。
sourceEvidence: A service client sends a request to a service server and waits for a response.

shortMeaningInSentence: get the result from a service call
example: The client should receive a response within the timeout period.
exampleZh: client 应该在 timeout 时间内 receive a response。
sourceEvidence: The service server processes the request and sends a response back to the calling client.

example: We should send an action goal for the navigation task.
exampleZh: 我们应该为 navigation task send an action goal。
sourceEvidence: An action client sends a goal to an action server to initiate a long-running task.

example: Cancel an action goal if the robot detects a new obstacle.
exampleZh: 如果检测到新 obstacle 就 cancel an action goal。
sourceEvidence: Actions support cancellation: a client can cancel a running goal at any time.

example: Check the message type before subscribing to the topic.
exampleZh: 在 subscribe topic 前先 check the message type。
sourceEvidence: Each ROS2 topic carries messages of a specific type defined in a .msg file.

example: Inspect the topic list to see if the camera driver is running.
exampleZh: Inspect the topic list 看看 camera driver 是否在运行。
sourceEvidence: The ros2 topic list command shows all topics currently active in the ROS2 system.

example: You need to spin the node to process incoming messages.
exampleZh: 你需要 spin the node 才能处理收到的消息。
sourceEvidence: Spinning a node runs its executor, which processes callbacks from subscriptions, services, and timers.

example: Use a callback group to handle concurrent sensor callbacks safely.
exampleZh: use a callback group 来安全处理并发的 sensor callback。
sourceEvidence: Callback groups control how callbacks are executed by the executor, allowing mutually exclusive or concurrent execution.

shortMeaningInSentence: rename a topic at launch time
example: Remap a topic in the launch file to match the sensor output name.
exampleZh: 在 launch file 里 remap a topic 以匹配 sensor 输出的名字。
sourceEvidence: Launch files support topic remapping so nodes can be connected without modifying source code.

shortMeaningInSentence: read and parse a launch configuration
example: Load a launch file that starts all the navigation nodes.
exampleZh: Load a launch file 来启动所有 navigation node。
sourceEvidence: ROS2 launch files define which nodes to start and how to configure them.

example: Run a launch file to bring up the full navigation stack.
exampleZh: Run a launch file 来启动整个 navigation stack。
sourceEvidence: The ros2 launch command runs a launch file that starts and configures multiple nodes.

shortMeaningInSentence: register a parameter in node code
example: Declare a parameter for the max velocity with a default value.
exampleZh: Declare a parameter 给 max velocity 设个默认值。
sourceEvidence: Nodes must declare parameters before using them, optionally providing default values.

shortMeaningInSentence: change a parameter value at runtime
example: Set a parameter at runtime to change the controller gain dynamically.
exampleZh: 在运行时 set a parameter 来动态修改 controller gain。
sourceEvidence: Parameters can be set at runtime using the ros2 param set command or the parameter API.

example: Configure the lifecycle node before activating it.
exampleZh: 在 activate 前先 configure the lifecycle node。
sourceEvidence: Managed nodes must be configured after creation before they can be activated.

example: Activate the lifecycle node after the hardware is ready.
exampleZh: 在硬件准备好后 activate the lifecycle node。
sourceEvidence: After configuration, managed nodes can be activated to begin processing data.

example: Deactivate the lifecycle node before switching hardware.
exampleZh: 在切换硬件前 deactivate the lifecycle node。
sourceEvidence: Managed nodes can be deactivated to pause processing without shutting down completely.

example: Log a warning if the sensor data is older than one second.
exampleZh: 如果 sensor 数据超过一秒就 log a warning。
sourceEvidence: ROS2 logging supports multiple severity levels including DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, and FATAL.

example: Record a bag file during the test to capture all sensor data.
exampleZh: 测试期间 record a bag file 来捕获所有 sensor 数据。
sourceEvidence: rosbag2 can record topic data to disk for later replay and offline analysis.

example: Replay a bag file to test the perception pipeline offline.
exampleZh: Replay a bag file 来离线测试 perception pipeline。
sourceEvidence: rosbag2 can replay recorded messages to reproduce system behavior for testing.

example: Load the component dynamically without restarting the process.
exampleZh: 动态 load the component 无需重启进程。
sourceEvidence: ROS2 composition allows loading node components into a single process for efficient communication.

shortMeaningInSentence: run multiple components in one process
example: Compose the node with the camera driver to reduce latency.
exampleZh: compose the node 与 camera driver 以降低 latency。
sourceEvidence: Composing multiple node components into one process avoids inter-process communication overhead.

term: The node is not publishing messages.
example: The node is not publishing messages. Check the log for errors.
exampleZh: The node is not publishing messages. The node is not publishing messages，检查 log 有没有报错。
sourceEvidence: Topics carry messages from publishers to subscribers; if no messages arrive the publisher may not be running.

term: The topic has the wrong message type.
example: The topic has the wrong message type. The subscriber expects a different .msg definition.
exampleZh: The topic has the wrong message type. The topic has the wrong message type，subscriber 期望的是另一种 .msg。
sourceEvidence: ROS2 topics use strongly typed messages; publisher and subscriber must use matching message types.

term: The service never returns a response.
example: The service never returns a response. The server might be stuck.
exampleZh: The service never returns a response. The service never returns a response，server 可能卡住了。
sourceEvidence: Service calls expect a response; if the server fails the client may block indefinitely.

term: The action goal does not finish.
example: The action goal does not finish. The server might be waiting for a condition.
exampleZh: The action goal does not finish. The action goal does not finish，server 可能在等某个条件。
sourceEvidence: Actions are long-running; a goal may not finish if the server cannot complete the requested task.

example: The parameter is not declared. The node will crash when trying to read it.
exampleZh: The parameter is not declared. The parameter is not declared，node 在读取时就会 crash。
sourceEvidence: Nodes must declare parameters before accessing them, otherwise a runtime error occurs.

term: The launch file cannot find the package.
example: The launch file cannot find the package. Is it installed in the workspace?
exampleZh: The launch file cannot find the package. The launch file cannot find the package，这个 package 装在工作区里了吗？
sourceEvidence: Launch files reference packages by name; the package must be installed in the workspace for the launch to succeed.

term: The bag file is missing this topic.
shortMeaning: bag file 缺少这个 topic
example: The bag file is missing this topic. It was not included in the recording.
exampleZh: The bag file is missing this topic. The bag file is missing this topic，录制时没有包含这个 topic。
sourceEvidence: rosbag2 records only the topics specified at recording time; unlisted topics will be missing from the bag.

term: The callback is not being called.
example: The callback is not being called. Check if the topic name matches.
exampleZh: The callback is not being called. The callback is not being called，检查 topic name 是否匹配。
sourceEvidence: Subscription callbacks are invoked when messages arrive; if the topic name or QoS does not match, callbacks will not fire.

term: The lifecycle node is not active.
shortMeaning: lifecycle node 不在 active 状态
example: The lifecycle node is not active. It needs to be configured and activated first.
exampleZh: The lifecycle node is not active. The lifecycle node is not active，需要先 configure 再 activate。
sourceEvidence: A managed node only processes data in the active state; other states do not run callbacks.

term: The publisher and subscriber are not connected.
shortMeaning: publisher 和 subscriber 没连上
example: The publisher and subscriber are not connected. Check the QoS settings.
exampleZh: The publisher and subscriber are not connected. The publisher and subscriber are not connected，检查一下 QoS 设置。
sourceEvidence: DDS establishes connections between compatible publishers and subscribers; incompatible QoS profiles prevent connection.

example: The odometry node should broadcast a transform from odom to base_link.
exampleZh: odometry node 应该 broadcast a transform 从 odom 到 base_link。
sourceEvidence: A tf2 broadcaster publishes coordinate frame transforms so other nodes can use them.

shortMeaningInSentence: query the latest transform between frames
example: Lookup a transform from the camera frame to the base frame.
exampleZh: Lookup a transform 从 camera frame 到 base frame。
sourceEvidence: A tf2 listener can look up the latest available transform between any two coordinate frames.

shortMeaningInSentence: move point cloud to another frame
example: Transform a point cloud from the sensor frame to the map frame.
exampleZh: transform a point cloud 从 sensor frame 到 map frame。
sourceEvidence: tf2 can transform point clouds and other data types between coordinate frames using the transform tree.

example: Set the frame id to base_link in the sensor message header.
exampleZh: 在 sensor message header 里 set the frame id 为 base_link。
sourceEvidence: Every message that has a position in space must include a frame id in its header for tf2 to interpret it.

example: Check the frame tree to see if all transforms are being broadcast.
exampleZh: Check the frame tree 看所有 transform 是否都在 broadcast。
sourceEvidence: The tf2 tree shows all connected coordinate frames and the transforms between them.

term: The transform is not available yet.
example: The transform is not available yet. Wait for the broadcaster to publish it.
exampleZh: The transform is not available yet. The transform is not available yet，等 broadcaster publish 它。
sourceEvidence: tf2 lookups can fail if the requested transform has not been broadcast yet or is too old.

example: The frame id is wrong. The point cloud says camera_link but it should be camera_frame.
exampleZh: The frame id is wrong. The frame id is wrong，point cloud 里写的是 camera_link 但应该是 camera_frame。
sourceEvidence: Mismatched frame ids between data and the tf2 tree are a common cause of transform lookup failures.

example: The timestamp is too old. The transform data has expired.
exampleZh: The timestamp is too old. The timestamp is too old，transform 数据已经过期了。
sourceEvidence: tf2 enforces a timeout on transform lookups; transforms older than the timeout are considered expired.

shortMeaningInSentence: refresh costmap with new sensor data
example: The local planner needs to update the costmap before computing a new path.
exampleZh: local planner 在计算新 path 前需要 update the costmap。
sourceEvidence: Costmaps are updated with each new sensor observation to reflect the current environment.

example: Clear the costmap around the robot after a rotation.
exampleZh: 旋转后 clear the costmap 在机器人周围。
sourceEvidence: Costmaps provide a clearing operation to remove outdated obstacle information from specific regions.

shortMeaningInSentence: expand obstacle footprint by robot radius
example: Inflate the obstacle by the robot radius in the costmap.
exampleZh: 在 costmap 里按 robot radius inflate the obstacle。
sourceEvidence: The inflation layer expands obstacles by the robot footprint radius to keep the robot at a safe distance.

shortMeaningInSentence: find a route from start to goal
example: The global planner should compute a path from the current pose to the goal.
exampleZh: global planner 应该从当前位置 compute a path 到 goal。
sourceEvidence: The planner server computes a path from the robot start pose to the goal pose using the global costmap.

shortMeaningInSentence: track a planned path with the controller
example: The controller should follow the path generated by the global planner.
exampleZh: controller 应该 follow the path 由 global planner 生成的。
sourceEvidence: The controller server uses the local planner to follow the global path while avoiding obstacles.

shortMeaningInSentence: send a goal pose to Nav2
example: Send a navigation goal to move the robot to the docking station.
exampleZh: Send a navigation goal 让机器人移动到 docking station。
sourceEvidence: Nav2 uses the NavigateToPose action to accept navigation goals from clients.

example: Cancel the navigation goal if the robot is stuck for too long.
exampleZh: 如果机器人卡住太久就 cancel the navigation goal。
sourceEvidence: Navigation goals can be cancelled through the action interface if the task needs to be stopped.

example: Check the goal tolerance if the robot stops too far from the target.
exampleZh: 如果机器人在目标前停得太远就 check the goal tolerance。
sourceEvidence: The goal checker verifies that the robot reaches within the configured tolerance of the goal pose.

example: Tune the controller to reduce oscillation around the path.
exampleZh: Tune the controller 以减少 path 附近的震荡。
sourceEvidence: Nav2 controllers expose parameters that can be tuned to improve path following performance.

example: Enable recovery behavior so the robot can escape when stuck.
exampleZh: enable recovery behavior 让机器人卡住时能逃出来。
sourceEvidence: Recovery behaviors help the robot recover from stuck situations by spinning or backing up.

example: Run a behavior tree that sequences planning, control, and recovery.
exampleZh: Run a behavior tree 来串联 planning、control 和 recovery。
sourceEvidence: Nav2 uses behavior trees to orchestrate the navigation pipeline from planning to recovery.

example: Dock the robot at the charging station after the task is complete.
exampleZh: 任务完成后 dock the robot 到 charging station。
sourceEvidence: Nav2 can navigate to a docking station with high precision using specialized approach behaviors.

term: The local costmap is not updating.
example: The local costmap is not updating. Check if the sensor topics are still publishing.
exampleZh: The local costmap is not updating. The local costmap is not updating，检查 sensor topic 是否还在 publish。
sourceEvidence: Costmaps depend on sensor data; if sensors stop publishing the costmap shows stale information.

term: The global planner returns an empty path.
example: The global planner returns an empty path. The goal might be in an unreachable area.
exampleZh: The global planner returns an empty path. The global planner returns an empty path，goal 可能在不可到达的区域。
sourceEvidence: The planner server may fail to find a path if the goal is in an unreachable or occupied location.

term: The controller cannot follow the path.
example: The controller cannot follow the path. The robot keeps deviating from the plan.
exampleZh: The controller cannot follow the path. The controller cannot follow the path，机器人不断偏离 plan。
sourceEvidence: The controller server may fail to track the path if gains are too low or the path has sharp turns.

term: The robot stops near the obstacle.
shortMeaning: 机器人在障碍物前停下了
example: The robot stops near the obstacle. The inflation might be too large.
exampleZh: The robot stops near the obstacle. The robot stops near the obstacle，inflation 可能太大了。
sourceEvidence: Costmap inflation can cause the robot to stop farther from obstacles than expected if configured too aggressively.

term: The goal tolerance is too strict.
example: The goal tolerance is too strict. The robot keeps adjusting near the goal.
exampleZh: The goal tolerance is too strict. The goal tolerance is too strict，机器人在 goal 附近不断微调。
sourceEvidence: Goal tolerance parameters determine how close the robot must get to the goal before navigation is considered complete.

term: The recovery behavior does not start.
example: The recovery behavior does not start. The behavior tree might be misconfigured.
exampleZh: The recovery behavior does not start. The recovery behavior does not start，behavior tree 可能配置错了。
sourceEvidence: Recovery behaviors are triggered by the behavior tree when the primary actions fail to make progress.

example: The behavior tree gets stuck. It never proceeds past planning.
exampleZh: The behavior tree gets stuck. 在 planning 后就不继续了。
sourceEvidence: Behavior trees can get stuck if a node never returns success or failure, blocking the rest of the tree.

term: The path is not smooth enough.
example: The path is not smooth enough. The controller generates jerky commands.
exampleZh: The path is not smooth enough. The path is not smooth enough，controller 生成了 jerky 的指令。
sourceEvidence: The velocity smoother enforces smooth acceleration limits; without it the controller output may be jerky.

term: The planner fails in narrow spaces.
shortMeaning: planner 在狭窄空间里失败
example: The planner fails in narrow spaces. The robot footprint might be set too large.
exampleZh: The planner fails in narrow spaces. The planner fails in narrow spaces，robot footprint 可能设得太大了。
sourceEvidence: Planners may fail to find paths when the robot footprint is too large for narrow corridors.

shortMeaning: 机器人 re-plan 太频繁了
example: The robot replans too often. The costmap might be too sensitive to sensor noise.
exampleZh: The robot replans too often. The robot replans too often，costmap 可能对 sensor noise 太敏感了。
sourceEvidence: Frequent replanning can be caused by noisy costmaps or overly sensitive obstacle detection.

example: Plan a motion from the current joint state to the target pose.
exampleZh: Plan a motion 从当前 joint state 到 target pose。
sourceEvidence: MoveIt2 motion planning computes collision-free trajectories from start to goal states.

example: Execute the trajectory after the planner returns a valid path.
exampleZh: planner 返回 valid path 后 execute the trajectory。
sourceEvidence: After planning, the trajectory is sent to the robot controller for execution.

example: Check for collisions before sending the trajectory to the robot.
exampleZh: 在发送 trajectory 到机器人前先 check for collisions。
sourceEvidence: Collision checking verifies that robot links do not collide with objects or themselves during motion.

example: Update the planning scene when a new object is detected.
exampleZh: 检测到新 object 时 update the planning scene。
sourceEvidence: The planning scene represents the robot and its environment; it must be updated to reflect new sensor data.

example: Solve inverse kinematics to find joint angles for the target pose.
exampleZh: Solve inverse kinematics 来找到 target pose 的 joint angle。
sourceEvidence: Inverse kinematics solvers compute the joint positions needed to achieve a desired end-effector pose.

shortMeaningInSentence: define the goal pose for the end-effector
example: Set a pose target for the end-effector above the object.
exampleZh: Set a pose target 让 end-effector 在 object 上方。
sourceEvidence: Motion planning requests include a target pose or joint state that the robot should reach.

shortMeaningInSentence: connect grasped object to robot link
example: Attach the object to the gripper link after grasping.
exampleZh: grasp 后 attach the object 到 gripper link。

example: Detach the object from the gripper after placing it on the table.
exampleZh: 把 object 放到桌上后 detach the object 从 gripper 上。

term: MoveIt cannot find a valid plan.
example: MoveIt cannot find a valid plan. The goal might be in collision.

sourceEvidence: Motion planning can fail if the goal is unreachable, in collision, or violates joint constraints.

term: The trajectory violates the joint limit.

sourceEvidence: Joint limits define the valid range of motion; planners must respect these bounds or trajectories will be rejected.

term: The planning scene is not updated.
example: The planning scene is not updated. The robot cannot see the new obstacle.
exampleZh: The planning scene is not updated. The planning scene is not updated，机器人看不到新的 obstacle。
sourceEvidence: The planning scene must be kept up to date with sensor data to avoid planning into obstacles.

example: The inverse kinematics solver fails. The target may be unreachable.
exampleZh: The inverse kinematics solver fails. 目标可能不可达。
sourceEvidence: IK solvers can fail if the target pose is unreachable or too close to a singularity.

term: The pose target is not reachable.
example: The pose target is not reachable. It lies outside the workspace.
exampleZh: The pose target is not reachable. 在 workspace 之外。
sourceEvidence: A pose target is reachable only if it lies within the robot workspace and satisfies all constraints.

example: Load the controller before switching to trajectory execution mode.
exampleZh: load the controller 在切换到 trajectory execution 模式前。
sourceEvidence: The controller manager loads controllers dynamically at runtime from available plugins.

example: Switch the controller from position mode to velocity mode.
exampleZh: Switch the controller 从 position mode 到 velocity mode。
sourceEvidence: The controller manager supports switching between loaded controllers at runtime.

example: Start the controller after the hardware interface is ready.
exampleZh: 在 hardware interface 准备好后 start the controller。
sourceEvidence: Loaded controllers must be started through the controller manager lifecycle to begin processing.

example: Stop the controller before unloading it from memory.
exampleZh: 在从内存中卸载前 stop the controller。
sourceEvidence: Controllers can be stopped to pause their operation before being switched or unloaded.

example: Read the state interface to get the latest encoder values.
exampleZh: Read the state interface 来获取最新的 encoder 值。
sourceEvidence: State interfaces provide sensor readings from hardware components to the controller.

example: Write the command interface to send velocity commands to the motors.
exampleZh: Write the command interface 来发送 velocity command 到电机。
sourceEvidence: Command interfaces carry actuator commands from the controller to the hardware.

example: Send position commands to each joint at the control loop rate.
exampleZh: 以 control loop 频率 send position commands 到每个 joint。
sourceEvidence: Position command interfaces let controllers command target joint positions to the hardware.

example: Configure the hardware with the correct communication port and baud rate.
exampleZh: Configure the hardware 用正确的 communication port 和 baud rate。
sourceEvidence: Hardware components must be configured with communication parameters before the controller can use them.

term: The controller cannot claim the interface.
example: The controller cannot claim the interface. Another controller might already own it.
exampleZh: The controller cannot claim the interface. The controller cannot claim the interface，可能另一个 controller 已经占用了。
sourceEvidence: Controllers claim exclusive access to hardware interfaces; conflicts occur if two controllers request the same interface.

term: The hardware interface is not ready.
example: The hardware interface is not ready. The robot might not be powered on.
exampleZh: The hardware interface is not ready. The hardware interface is not ready，机器人可能还没上电。
sourceEvidence: Hardware interfaces must be initialized and configured before they can accept commands or provide state data.

example: The command interface is missing. The hardware plugin did not export it.
exampleZh: The command interface is missing. The command interface is missing，hardware plugin 没有 export 它。
sourceEvidence: Hardware plugins must export command interfaces; missing interfaces prevent controllers from sending commands.

term: The state interface returns stale data.
example: The state interface returns stale data. The encoder read might be failing.
exampleZh: The state interface returns stale data. The state interface returns stale data，encoder read 可能失败了。
sourceEvidence: State interfaces should return the latest sensor readings; stale data indicates a communication problem.

term: The control loop is too slow.
example: The control loop is too slow. It should run at 1 kHz but only reaches 200 Hz.
exampleZh: The control loop is too slow. The control loop is too slow，应该 1 kHz 但实际只有 200 Hz。
sourceEvidence: ros2_control control loops must meet their target update rate for stable real-time control.

example: Filter the point cloud to remove points outside the region of interest.
exampleZh: Filter the point cloud 来移除感兴趣区域之外的点。
sourceEvidence: PCL provides PassThrough, VoxelGrid, and StatisticalOutlierRemoval filters to clean point cloud data.

example: Downsample the point cloud with a voxel grid before registration.
exampleZh: 在 registration 前用 voxel grid downsample the point cloud。
sourceEvidence: VoxelGrid downsampling reduces point cloud density to speed up downstream processing.

example: Segment the object from the table plane using RANSAC.
exampleZh: 用 RANSAC segment the object 从 table plane 上。
sourceEvidence: RANSAC-based plane segmentation separates objects from supporting surfaces like tables and floors.

example: Cluster the points into separate object candidates after segmentation.
exampleZh: cluster the points segmentation 后把点 cluster 成单独的 object candidate。
sourceEvidence: Euclidean clustering groups points within a distance threshold into distinct object clusters.

example: Register the point cloud to the reference map using ICP alignment.
exampleZh: register the point cloud 用 ICP 对齐到 reference map。
sourceEvidence: ICP registration aligns a source point cloud to a target by minimizing point-to-point distances.

example: Extract features from the point cloud for object recognition.
exampleZh: Extract features 从 point cloud 中用于 object recognition。
sourceEvidence: PCL can extract normals, keypoints, and descriptors like FPFH and SHOT from point clouds.

example: Calibrate the camera before running any visual perception pipeline.
exampleZh: 在运行任何 visual perception pipeline 前先 calibrate the camera。
sourceEvidence: Camera calibration estimates intrinsic parameters and distortion coefficients for accurate perception.

example: Remove outliers before feeding the point cloud to the registration algorithm.
exampleZh: 在把 point cloud 送入 registration 算法前先 remove outliers。
sourceEvidence: Statistical outlier removal deletes points that are far from their neighbors, reducing noise.

term: The point cloud is too noisy.
example: The point cloud is too noisy. Apply a statistical outlier filter first.
exampleZh: The point cloud is too noisy. The point cloud is too noisy，先 apply 一个 statistical outlier filter。
sourceEvidence: Noisy point clouds contain unwanted outlier points that degrade registration and segmentation results.

term: The object is not segmented correctly.
example: The object is not segmented correctly. The plane removal threshold might be too high.
exampleZh: The object is not segmented correctly. The object is not segmented correctly，plane removal threshold 可能太高了。
sourceEvidence: Segmentation quality depends on parameters like distance threshold; incorrect values cause under- or over-segmentation.

example: The camera calibration is off. The depth and RGB images are misaligned.
exampleZh: The camera calibration is off. The camera calibration is off，depth 和 RGB image 对不上。
sourceEvidence: Incorrect calibration parameters cause misalignment between depth and color data.

example: The registration result is unstable. It varies across frames.
exampleZh: The registration result is unstable. 逐帧变化很大。
sourceEvidence: ICP registration can produce unstable results if the initial alignment is poor or the point cloud is too sparse.

term: The depth image is missing data.
example: The depth image is missing data. Reflective surfaces cause holes.
exampleZh: The depth image is missing data. 反射面造成了空洞。
sourceEvidence: Depth cameras can have missing data in occluded regions or on reflective surfaces.

term: The tracking fails in low light.
example: The tracking fails in low light. The feature detector cannot find enough points.
exampleZh: The tracking fails in low light. The tracking fails in low light，feature detector 找不到足够的点。
sourceEvidence: Visual feature tracking degrades in low-light conditions due to fewer detectable keypoints.

example: Estimate the pose by fusing wheel odometry with IMU readings.
exampleZh: 用 wheel odometry 和 IMU estimate the pose。
sourceEvidence: robot_localization fuses multiple sensor sources to estimate the robot pose via EKF.

example: Match the scan against the reference map for localization.
exampleZh: match the scan 对照 reference map 来进行 localization。
sourceEvidence: Scan matching aligns laser scans to a reference map or previous scan to estimate motion.

example: Close the loop to reduce accumulated drift in the map.
exampleZh: Close the loop 以减少地图中的累积 drift。
sourceEvidence: Loop closure detects when the robot returns to a previously visited location and corrects the map.

example: Optimize the pose graph after adding a loop closure constraint.
exampleZh: 在添加 loop closure constraint 后 optimize the pose graph。
sourceEvidence: Pose graph optimization adjusts all robot poses to be globally consistent after loop closure.

example: Build the map of the environment during the exploration phase.
exampleZh: 在 exploration 阶段 build the map of the environment。
sourceEvidence: SLAM simultaneously builds a map of the environment and localizes the robot within it.

example: Localize the robot before starting any autonomous task.
exampleZh: 在开始任何自主任务前 localize the robot。
sourceEvidence: AMCL localizes the robot within a known map using a particle filter and laser scan matching.

example: Save the map after the SLAM session is complete.
exampleZh: SLAM 结束后 save the map。
sourceEvidence: slam_toolbox supports saving generated maps for later use in localization-only mode.

example: Load the map before starting localization-only navigation.
exampleZh: 在启动 localization-only navigation 前 load the map。
sourceEvidence: Nav2 loads a pre-built occupancy grid map from the map server for localization and planning.

example: The pose estimate keeps drifting. The IMU might need recalibration.
exampleZh: The pose estimate keeps drifting. The pose estimate keeps drifting，IMU 可能需要重新 calibrate。
sourceEvidence: Without correction from absolute sensors, odometry-based pose estimates accumulate drift over time.

example: The scan matching fails here. The corridor has no distinctive features.
exampleZh: The scan matching fails here. The scan matching fails here，这个走廊没有任何 distinctive feature。
sourceEvidence: Scan matching degrades in featureless environments like long corridors where all scans look similar.

term: The loop closure is not detected.
shortMeaning: loop closure 没有被检测到
example: The loop closure is not detected. The search radius might be too small.
exampleZh: The loop closure is not detected. The loop closure is not detected，search radius 可能太小了。
sourceEvidence: Loop closure detection depends on the search radius and visual similarity between current and past observations.

term: The pose graph is not optimized.
example: The pose graph is not optimized. The loop closure constraint was not applied.
exampleZh: The pose graph is not optimized. The pose graph is not optimized，loop closure constraint 没有被 apply。
sourceEvidence: Pose graph optimization runs after adding loop closure edges; without it the graph is not globally consistent.

example: The robot cannot localize itself. The initial pose estimate might be wrong.
exampleZh: The robot cannot localize itself. The robot cannot localize itself，initial pose estimate 可能不对。
sourceEvidence: AMCL requires a reasonable initial pose estimate near the true robot location to converge.

example: Spawn the robot at the origin of the Gazebo world.
exampleZh: 在 Gazebo world 的原点 spawn the robot。
sourceEvidence: Robots and objects can be spawned into a running Gazebo simulation at specified positions.

example: Load the world file before spawning any robot models.
exampleZh: 在 spawn robot model 前 load the world。
sourceEvidence: Gazebo worlds define the simulation environment including static models, lighting, and physics.

example: Bridge the topic between Gazebo Transport and ROS2 middleware.
exampleZh: bridge the topic 在 Gazebo Transport 和 ROS2 之间。
sourceEvidence: ros_gz_bridge bridges messages between Gazebo Transport topics and ROS2 topics.

example: Reset the simulation before running the next test case.
exampleZh: 在运行下一个 test case 前 reset the simulation。
sourceEvidence: Gazebo supports resetting the simulation to its initial state for repeated testing.

example: Step the simulation forward frame by frame to debug the controller.
exampleZh: Step the simulation 逐帧前进以 debug controller。
sourceEvidence: Gazebo can step the simulation at a configured update rate, or advance one step at a time for debugging.

example: Deploy to the real robot after all simulation tests pass.
exampleZh: simulation 全部通过后 deploy to the real robot。
sourceEvidence: Nav2 can be deployed on real robots after validation in simulation environments.

term: The simulation works but the real robot fails.
example: The simulation works but the real robot fails. The sim-to-real gap is too large.
exampleZh: The simulation works but the real robot fails. The simulation works but the real robot fails，sim-to-real gap 太大了。
sourceEvidence: Differences between simulation and reality can cause behaviors that work in simulation to fail on real hardware.

term: Gazebo does not load the model.
example: Gazebo does not load the model. Check the SDF file path.
exampleZh: Gazebo does not load the model. Gazebo does not load the model，检查一下 SDF file path。
sourceEvidence: Model loading failures in Gazebo often result from missing or incorrectly referenced SDF model files.

example: The plugin is not loaded. The shared library might be missing from the plugin path.
exampleZh: The plugin is not loaded. The plugin is not loaded，shared library 可能不在 plugin path 里。
sourceEvidence: Gazebo plugins are loaded from shared libraries; missing or incorrectly referenced plugin paths cause load failures.

term: The simulated sensor data looks wrong.
shortMeaning: simulated sensor 数据看起来不对
example: The simulated sensor data looks wrong. The noise model might be incorrect.
exampleZh: The simulated sensor data looks wrong. The simulated sensor data looks wrong，noise model 可能不对。
sourceEvidence: Gazebo simulates sensors with configurable noise models; incorrect parameters produce unrealistic data.

example: Record a rosbag to reproduce the issue offline.
exampleZh: 录一个 rosbag 来 reproduce the issue 离线。
sourceEvidence: rosbag2 records data to help reproduce and debug issues that are difficult to trigger live.

example: Profile the latency from sensor input to control output.
exampleZh: Profile the latency 从 sensor input 到 control output。
sourceEvidence: ROS2 supports tracing tools to profile callback execution and end-to-end latency.

example: Measure the success rate of the planner over 100 test runs.
exampleZh: Measure the success rate of the planner 在 100 次测试运行中。
sourceEvidence: Robotics benchmarks evaluate algorithms using metrics like success rate, accuracy, and computation time.

example: Compare the baseline planner against the proposed method.
exampleZh: compare the baseline planner 和提出的新方法。
sourceEvidence: Benchmark studies compare proposed methods against established baselines on standard metrics.

example: Run the benchmark on three different datasets for a fair comparison.
exampleZh: Run the benchmark 在三个不同的 dataset 上做公平对比。
sourceEvidence: Standardized benchmarks like KITTI and EuRoC are used to evaluate SLAM system performance.

example: Evaluate the model on a held-out test set after training.
exampleZh: 在 training 后用 held-out test set evaluate the model。
sourceEvidence: Models are evaluated on separate test data to measure generalization performance.

example: Validate the result by comparing with ground truth measurements.
exampleZh: validate the result 通过与 ground truth 对比。
sourceEvidence: Testing and validation are part of the ROS2 package development workflow to ensure correctness.

example: Record the failure case in a rosbag for offline analysis.
exampleZh: Record the failure case 到一个 rosbag 中用于离线分析。
sourceEvidence: rosbag2 can be used to record specific scenarios, including failure cases, for debugging.

example: Analyze the log to find which node crashed first.
exampleZh: Analyze the log 来找出哪个 node 先 crash 的。
sourceEvidence: ROS2 logs contain severity-tagged messages that help analyze system behavior and diagnose failures.

example: Replay the dataset through the new perception pipeline for evaluation.
exampleZh: Replay the dataset 通过新的 perception pipeline 来做评估。
sourceEvidence: rosbag2 replay enables running algorithms on recorded data for consistent evaluation across experiments.

example: Test on the real robot to confirm the simulation results.
exampleZh: test on the real robot 来确认 simulation 的结果。
sourceEvidence: Nav2 should be tested on the real robot after simulation validation to confirm behavior.

example: Can you reproduce the issue? I cannot trigger it on my machine.
exampleZh: Can you reproduce the issue? 我在我的机器上无法触发。
sourceEvidence: Reproducing issues with rosbag data is a standard debugging practice in ROS2 development.

term: The log shows a timing problem.
shortMeaning: log 显示有 timing 问题
example: The log shows a timing problem. The callback is taking longer than the period.
exampleZh: The log shows a timing problem. The log shows a timing problem，callback 耗时超过了 period。
sourceEvidence: ROS2 logs include timestamps that help identify timing issues such as missed deadlines.

example: The latency is too high. The control loop misses its deadline.
exampleZh: The latency is too high. control loop 错过了 deadline。

example: The benchmark result is unstable. The success rate varies from 70 to 90 percent.
exampleZh: The benchmark result is unstable. The benchmark result is unstable，success rate 在 70% 到 90% 之间波动。
sourceEvidence: Benchmark results can be unstable due to stochastic algorithms or varying initial conditions.

term: The baseline fails in this scene.
shortMeaning: baseline 在这个场景中失败
example: The baseline fails in this scene. The corridors are too narrow.
exampleZh: The baseline fails in this scene. 走廊太窄了。
sourceEvidence: Baseline methods often fail on challenging scenarios that expose their limitations.

term: The failure case happens near obstacles.
shortMeaning: fail case 发生在 obstacle 附近
example: The failure case happens near obstacles. The planner might be too conservative.
exampleZh: The failure case happens near obstacles. The failure case happens near obstacles，planner 可能太保守了。
sourceEvidence: Navigation failures often occur near obstacles where the costmap constraints are tightest.

example: The result is not reproducible. The planner behaves differently each run.
exampleZh: The result is not reproducible. The result is not reproducible，planner 每次运行行为都不一样。

shortMeaning: 我们应该在真机上测试这个
example: The simulation looks good. We should test this on the real robot.
exampleZh: We should test this on the real robot. simulation 看起来没问题，we should test this on the real robot。
